paper_title: Extraction and Analysis of Phenolic Compounds from Betula and Pinus Pollen

methods.description: This method involves using ultrasonic shaking to extract free phenolic compounds from Betula pendula and Pinus sylvestris pollen. It evaluates the efficiency of phenolic compound extraction using different durations of ultrasonic shaking.

steps.description: Shake samples for 10 min using a shaker power of 113 W.
steps.searchable_summary: perform ultrasonic shaking of pollen samples for 10 minutes at 113 W

methods.method_name: Orbital Shaking Extraction of Phenolic Compounds
methods.description: This method uses orbital shaking to extract free phenolic compounds from Betula pendula and Pinus sylvestris pollen. It compares the efficiency of extraction with and without liquid nitrogen treatment.

steps.description: Process pollen with liquid nitrogen before extraction by orbital shaking.
steps.searchable_summary: treat pollen with liquid nitrogen before orbital shaking

steps.description: Perform orbital shaking to extract phenolic compounds.
steps.searchable_summary: perform orbital shaking to extract phenolic compounds

methods.description: This method uses High-Performance Liquid Chromatography (HPLC) to analyze the variety and concentration of phenolic compounds in Betula and Pinus pollen extracts.

steps.description: Analyze phenolic compounds using HPLC with a diode array detector.
steps.searchable_summary: perform HPLC analysis with diode array detector on pollen extracts

methods.description: This method involves using High-Performance Thin Layer Chromatography (HPTLC) to qualitatively analyze phenolic compounds in Betula and Pinus pollen extracts.

steps.description: Perform HPTLC analysis using a mobile phase and visualize under UV light.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct HPTLC analysis and visualize under UV light
